classical-latin.scholarly_consensus: High-confidence reconstruction (Allen Vox Latina standard, supported by grammarians like Quintilian/Cicero, poetic metrics, inscriptions, Romance descendants; minor updates via Calabrese for vowel qualities).

pronunciation_variants.classical: Golden Age (1st c. BCE): phonemic vowel length, V=/w/, C/G always hard /k g/, aspirates in loans, dynamic stress, trilled R, final M nasalizes/elides
pronunciation_variants.ecclesiastical: Italianate Church: V=[v], soft C/G before e/i ([tʃ dʒ]), AE=[ɛ], no quantity distinction, stress often marked, full final consonants

vowels: Short: /a [ä]/, /e [ɛ]/, /i [ɪ]/, /o [ɔ]/, /u [ʊ]/; Long: /aː [äː]/, /eː [eː]/, /iː [iː]/, /oː [oː]/, /uː [uː]/ (+ /y yː/ loans). Quality: shorts more open (Allen); Calabrese debate favors quantity-over-quality for some shorts.
consonants: Stops: /p t k b d g/ (+ labialized /kʷ gʷ/ for qu/gu; aspirated variants [pʰ etc.] in Greek loans); Fricatives: /f s h/; Nasals /m n/ (final m → nasalization); Liquids /r [r trill]/ /l (clear/dark)/; Approximants /j w/ (V=U-cons as /w/).
phoneme_inventory.quantity_system: Phonemic long/short vowels + geminates critical for syllable weight/meter (heavy = long vowel/diphthong or cons.-closed).
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: No soft c/g; V bilabial approximant; educated h pronounced; r always vibrant.

ipa_reconstructed.sample_text: Arma virumque cano, Troiae qui primus ab oris
ipa_reconstructed.transcription: [ˈar.ma wɪˈrũː.kʷɛ ˈkaː.noː ˈtroː.jaj kʷiː ˈpriː.mʊs ab ˈoː.riːs]
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Strongly attested via metrics/grammarians; minor disputes on short high vowel openness & aspiration degree.

classical-latin.stress_rules: Penultimate law: heavy penult (long vowel/diphthong or cons.-closed) stressed; otherwise antepenult (e.g., caˈno vs ˈvirumque).

prosody.rhythm: Quantitative (syllable weight) for dactylic hexameter; elision common.
prosody.pitch: Primarily stress (intensity); earlier pitch elements possible but not dominant.
prosody.pacing: Long vowels ~1.5–2× short duration; quantitative meter pacing.

classical-latin.grok_imagine_guidance: Key: bilabial /w/ (lips rounded, not teeth-on-lip v); trilled/rolled R; hold long vowels distinctly (sustained for meter); mild puff on aspirates (ph/th/ch loans); nasalize vowel before final -m (arma ~[ar.mã]); crisp shorts vs. sustained longs; lip rounding for u/o/w; avoid English reductions/schwas; breath groups by phrase for natural flow.
tutoring_hooks: Latin V = /w/ ('weni widi wiki' for Caesar) — not modern v!
C/G always hard /k g/ ('Kikero' for Cicero) — no soft sounds.
Vowel length is phonemic & changes meter/meaning (liber 'book' vs. līber 'free').
Final M often nasalizes/elides (poetic flow secret).

ancient-greek.scholarly_consensus: Attic Classical core from Allen Vox Graeca + metrics/inscriptions/grammarians; high on stops/vowels/quantity/pitch; Koine shifts well-attested via papyri.

pronunciation_variants.attic: 5th–4th BCE: aspirated stops [pʰ tʰ kʰ], voiced stops [b d g], /y/ upsilon, pitch accent, full quantity.
pronunciation_variants.koine: Hellenistic: spirantization begins (/pʰ/→/f/ etc.), iotacism starts, h lost.
pronunciation_variants.byzantine: Medieval: full stress accent, iotacism complete, Modern-like vowels/fricatives.

vowels: Short: /a e i y o/; Long: /aː ɛː eː iː yː ɔː uː/ (+ diphthongs ai/au/eu/oi etc.; ei/ou monophthongize early). Quantity moraic.
consonants: Stops: /p t k pʰ tʰ kʰ b d g/; Fricatives /s h/; Nasals /m n/; Liquids /r (trilled, initial voiceless possible) l/; Semivowels /j w/ (lost in Attic text). Zeta [zd] or [dz] debate.
phoneme_inventory.quantity_system: Phonemic length (vowels + geminates); morae for accent/meter.
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: Three-way stop contrast; rough/smooth breathing (h vs zero); pitch accent phonemic.

ipa_reconstructed.confidence: high (85–90% core consonants/vowels/quantity; 70% exact pitch contour)
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Vowels/stops/quantity strongly attested; pitch via grammarians (reconstructed melodic); Homeric w/digamma metrical but text-silent; zeta/iotacism timing disputed.

ancient-greek.stress_rules: Pitch accent: one high-tone mora per word (acute = high/rising; circumflex = falling on long; grave = default/low or sandhi).

prosody.pitch: Musical contour (high tone on accented mora); practical recreation as slight rise/fall.
prosody.pacing: Longs/diphthongs double mora; aspirates breathy release.
prosody.pitch_accent_note: Not English stress; changes to stress accent post-Classical.

ancient-greek.grok_imagine_guidance: Strong breathy puff on aspirates (phi= p+h not f; theta t+h; chi k+h); upsilon rounded [y] (French tu); beta pure [b]; hold longs/diphthongs 2× duration; musical pitch contour on accents (sing rising/falling); trilled rho; pure vowels (no English diphthong creep); rough breathing initial [h]; timing for hexameter flow with caesura breaths.
tutoring_hooks: Phi/Theta/Chi = aspirated stops (puff of air after p/t/k) — not modern f/th/ch sounds in Attic!
Pitch accent = musical tones (high/fall), not stress — like singing!
Eta η = long open [ɛː] (evolves to i later via iotacism).
Zeta ζ often [zd] in Attic (like 'wisdom' z).
